headline: Oil Palm Replanting Fund Faces RM1.8B Shortfall
context: MPOB reported that the oil palm replanting fund faces a RM1.8 billion shortfall to finance the replacement of 420,000 hectares of ageing palms past their 25-year productive life, threatening future production and smallholder income.

cards.big: 420,000 hectares of oil palms past prime — RM1.8 billion replanting shortfall means yields falling 22% on ageing trees
cards.sub: Smallholders face 3-year income gap during replanting with no guaranteed replacement income

cards.big: Ageing palms yield 15 tonnes per hectare versus 22 tonnes for replanted stock — RM12 billion in annual revenue at risk
cards.sub: Indonesia's replanting programme at RM3.2 billion annually dwarfs Malaysia's RM800 million — competitiveness gap widening

cards.big: 112,000 smallholders manage 42% of palm oil acreage but access only 18% of replanting fund disbursements
cards.sub: Large plantation companies replant on schedule while smallholders delay 5-8 years because they can't survive the income gap

cards.big: MPOB replanting fund levy at RM10 per tonne hasn't been adjusted since 2005 despite CPO prices tripling
cards.sub: Fund governance allows RM180 million in annual administrative costs — 22% of total collections consumed by bureaucracy

cards.big: Neglecting replanting is eating the seed corn — every year of delay means lower yields, less income, and a declining industry
Should the replanting levy be tripled to RM30 per tonne with 80% earmarked directly for smallholder income support during replanting?
